response:
To find the **greatest common divisor (GCD)** of the set $\{196, 225, 256\}$, we follow these steps:

---

### Step 1: Find the GCD of the first two numbers

We start with:
$$
\gcd(196, 225)
$$

Use the Euclidean algorithm:

$$
\begin{align*}
225 \div 196 &= 1 \text{ remainder } 29 \\
196 \div 29 &= 6 \text{ remainder } 22 \\
29 \div 22 &= 1 \text{ remainder } 7 \\
22 \div 7 &= 3 \text{ remainder } 1 \\
7 \div 1 &= 7 \text{ remainder } 0 \\
\end{align*}
$$

So,
$$
\gcd(196, 225) = 1
$$

---

### Step 2: Find the GCD of the result with the third number

Now find:
$$
\gcd(1, 256)
$$

Since the GCD of any number and 1 is 1, we have:
$$
\gcd(1, 256) = 1
$$

---

### Final Answer:

$$
\boxed{1}
$$
